writer.last_problem: The chapter’s central stakes are explained in a clunky exposition dump (vision of static manuscript, Orin’s fate) rather than being felt in the moment; the danger feels abstract and the characters never experience the imminent threat.

must_fix: Show collapse tightening in real time—words solidifying, breath turning to ink, agents feeling the threat; replace generic lullaby with a vivid, sensory memory that physically tears from Elisa’s mind; reduce placeholder tags to only critical meta‑aware moments; give each supporting agent a distinct, costly reaction during the binding rite; integrate the Hall of Mirrors collapse into a single, vivid image and record it as a collapsed artifact.
